description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Save three beginner-level safety articles that are 8 minutes or shorter to read, rated at least 4.0 stars, to your Favorites.

target_names: Beginner's Guide to Life Jackets and PFDs
7 Essential Right-of-Way Rules for New Boaters
Docking Without Drama: Basic Dock Maneuvers for Beginners

tasks.instruction: Find a pontoon safety checklist for a 4–8 hour day trip with 4–6 passengers, mark the first five checklist items as completed, and save the checklist.

checklist_item_texts_completed: Check detailed weather forecast and thunderstorm risk for the entire day trip window.
Confirm passenger count (4–6 people) and verify it does not exceed the boat’s capacity plate.
Ensure a properly sized, USCG-approved life jacket is available and in good condition for each passenger.
Test navigation lights, horn, and any installed VHF radio or mobile communication devices.
Verify fuel level is sufficient for the trip plus a safety reserve and check for fuel or oil leaks.
